Jas.2.5: Hath not God chosen the poor of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom? Divine election runs against worldly criteria. Faith-wealth eclipses material wealth.
Prov.16.9: A man's heart deviseth his way, but the LORD directeth his steps. Human planning, divine determination. The verse balances human agency with sovereign providence.
John.1.18: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, He hath declared (exēgēsato — exegeted) Him. Christ is the divine exegesis. He has explained the unseen God by being Him.
Col.1.16: By Him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible. All things were created by Him, and for Him. Christ as Creator and goal of creation.
Isa.65.24: Before they call, I will answer; and while they are yet speaking, I will hear. Eschatological intimacy. God's responsiveness anticipates prayer.
1John.3.2: When He shall appear, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is. Glorification follows beatific vision. The vision transforms.
Eph.5.2: Walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given Himself for us, an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour. The cross as the sustained pattern of Christian love.
Isa.6.9: Hear ye indeed, but understand not — judicial hardening. Cited by Jesus (Matt 13:14) when explaining the parables. Divine judgment includes giving people what they want.
Rev.21.8: The fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable... shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death. Eschatological exclusion from the new creation. Eight categories named.
Ps.34.18: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart, and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit. God's proximity is to the crushed, not the proud. Brokenness is the venue of salvation.
2Pet.3.9: The Lord is not slack concerning His promise — divine timing is not delay. Longsuffering, not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance. The patience is purposeful.
Jer.17.10: I the LORD search the heart, I try the reins, even to give every man according to his ways. Divine omniscience for moral assessment. Judgment proceeds from comprehensive knowledge.
Isa.66.2: To this man will I look, even to him that is poor and of a contrite spirit, and trembleth at my word. Three traits attract divine attention: poverty of spirit, contrition, reverence for Scripture.
2Cor.5.10: We must all appear before the judgment seat (bēma) of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body. Believers' deeds judged for reward, not condemnation (Rom 8:1).
Luke.10.27: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself. The lawyer's correct summary. Both tables of the Decalogue compressed.
1John.1.9: If we confess (homologōmen — agree with God about) our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness. Confession is pre-condition; forgiveness is justly grounded in the cross.
1Pet.1.6: Wherein ye greatly rejoice, though now for a season ye are in heaviness through manifold temptations. Joy and grief co-exist for the Christian; trials are brief and purposeful.
Matt.12.34: Out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh. The mouth is heart-revelation. Speech is symptomatic, not causative.
Col.1.27: Christ in you, the hope of glory. The greatest mystery: the indwelling Christ as the down-payment of consummated glory. Personal indwelling, present tense, eschatological orientation.
Rom.10.9: If thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved. Confession (objective fact: Jesus is Lord) + faith (subjective trust in resurrection).
Matt.25.34: Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world. Election language at the eschatological enthronement. The kingdom is pre-prepared.
Rom.8.11: If the Spirit of Him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, He shall also quicken your mortal bodies by His Spirit. Resurrection power is the Spirit's promise to indwelt believers.
Exod.15.26: I am the LORD that healeth thee. Yahweh-Rapha — one of the covenant names. Conditional on obedience; the principle of moral health, applied also to physical.
Gal.5.16: Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh. Spirit-walking is the antidote to flesh-fulfillment. The two are mutually exclusive in any given moment.
Jer.32.27: Behold, I am the LORD, the God of all flesh: is there anything too hard (yipale') for me? Rhetorical assertion of omnipotence. Echoes Gen 18:14.
Mal.3.10: Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse... prove me now herewith, saith the LORD of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven. Tithing test. The verse is OT covenantal; the principle of generous giving with God's overflowing supply remains.
2Cor.3.17: Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty. Spirit-presence produces freedom — from law's condemnation, from self, from fear.
1Pet.2.25: Ye were as sheep going astray; but are now returned unto the Shepherd and Bishop (episkopon — overseer) of your souls. Christ as both pastoral guide and oversight authority.
Gen.28.15: I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest. The covenant promise to Jacob at Bethel. Comprehensive divine companionship.
Prov.3.9: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase. Material giving as covenantal honor. The firstfruits principle.
Isa.49.23: They shall not be ashamed that wait for me. Eschatological vindication of God's people. The waiting is rewarded with public honor.
Jer.10.14: Every man is brutish (ba'ar — stupid) in his knowledge: every founder is confounded by the graven image; for his molten image is falsehood. Idolatry is the bankruptcy of human wisdom.
Col.4.6: Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man. Christian speech: gracious in tone, preserving in effect, contextually wise.
John.6.40: This is the will of Him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son and believeth on Him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day. The Father's will, the Son's promise, eschatological resurrection.
Ps.110.1: The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool. The most-quoted OT verse in the NT. Christ cites it (Matt 22:44) to confirm His own deity.
Exod.19.5: If ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure (segullah) unto me above all people. Conditional covenant identity. Cited 1 Pet 2:9 of the church.
Jas.5.7: Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth. The farmer's patience as Christian model.
Isa.55.1: Ho, every one that thirsteth, come ye to the waters... come, buy and eat; yea, come, buy wine and milk without money and without price. Free salvation invitation. The auctioneer's call to the destitute.
Luke.24.44: All things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me. Christ's threefold canonical claim — He is the subject of the OT.
Col.3.5: Mortify (nekrōsate — kill, put to death) therefore your members which are upon the earth. Five vices listed. Sanctification involves decisive killing of sin, not negotiation.
Zeph.3.17: The LORD thy God in the midst of thee is mighty; He will save, He will rejoice over thee with joy; He will rest in His love, He will joy over thee with singing. The astonishing portrait of God singing over His people.
Matt.6.24: No man can serve two masters... Ye cannot serve God and mammon (mamōn — material wealth). Categorical exclusivity. Money as a rival deity.
Ps.27.4: One thing have I desired of the LORD, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life. The simplified prayer of focused devotion.
Acts.20.28: Take heed unto yourselves, and to all the flock... feed the church of God, which He hath purchased with His own blood. Pastoral charge to the Ephesian elders. Particular redemption stated explicitly.
John.1.4: In Him was life; and the life was the light of men. The Word as life-source and light-source for humanity. Verse 5: the light shines in darkness, undefeated.
2Pet.1.17: He received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice... This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Peter's eyewitness of the Transfiguration.
Jer.31.31: Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel. Cited Heb 8:8–12. The new covenant inaugurated at the Last Supper (Luke 22:20).
Rev.21.4: God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain. The new creation's negative inventory — what is removed.
Hab.2.3: For the vision is yet for an appointed time... though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come. Eschatological waiting. Cited Heb 10:37 of Christ's coming.
Rom.8.15: Ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption (huiothesias), whereby we cry, Abba, Father. Adoption produces filial intimacy with God.
Ps.139.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts. David's invitation to divine inspection. The prayer of the believer with nothing to hide.
Heb.10.36: Ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise. Endurance bridges obedience and reward.
Rev.7.14: These are they which came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb. Tribulation saints, cleansed by atoning blood. The paradox: blood whitens.
Acts.10.38: God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good. Trinitarian Christology in Peter's sermon. The Spirit-anointed mission of Jesus.
Isa.40.3: The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD. Cited in all four Gospels of John the Baptist. Messianic forerunner prophecy.
Matt.10.22: Ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake; but he that endureth to the end shall be saved. Persecution prophesied; perseverance is the marker of genuine faith.
Deut.28.1: It shall come to pass, if thou shalt hearken diligently unto the voice of the LORD thy God... the LORD thy God will set thee on high above all nations. The Mosaic blessings conditional on obedience.
John.3.5: Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God. Water = spiritual cleansing (Ezek 36:25); Spirit = regeneration. Both are facets of one new birth.
1John.5.11: This is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in His Son. Eternal life is located in Christ; have the Son, have the life.
Rev.12.11: They overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of their testimony; and they loved not their lives unto the death. Three weapons against the accuser: cross, confession, courage.
John.3.15: Whosoever believeth in Him should not perish, but have eternal life. Cited Numbers 21 (the brazen serpent) as Christ's typological self-reference (v. 14). Faith looks; faith lives.
Rev.2.7: To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the tree of life. Eden restored as eschatological reward to perseverers. The seven letters' promises to overcomers.
Prov.4.18: The path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day. Progressive sanctification pictured. The Christian life is sunrise — increasing brightness.
Matt.22.37: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind. The first and great commandment. Total-faculty love.
1John.3.22: Whatsoever we ask, we receive of Him, because we keep His commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in His sight. Effective prayer flows from obedient lives.
Jas.3.13: Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? Let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom. Wisdom is demonstrated, not asserted.
Ps.119.105: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path. Scripture as immediate (lamp) and longer-range (light) guidance.
Ps.34.8: O taste and see that the LORD is good. Experiential invitation. Theology requires personal sampling, not just propositional assent.
2Tim.2.15: Study (spoudason — be diligent) to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing (orthotomounta — cutting straight) the word of truth. Pastoral hermeneutics.
Ps.40.5: Many, O LORD my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done. Innumerable divine acts of providence. They cannot be reckoned up in order — beyond cataloguing.
Ps.100.3: Know ye that the LORD He is God: it is He that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are His people, and the sheep of His pasture. Creation, possession, pastoral care — three theological pillars.
Titus.1.2: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie (apseudēs), promised before the world began. Pre-temporal divine promise to the elect, guaranteed by God's nature.
Rom.13.14: Put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof. Christ as garment; flesh-provisioning is incompatible. Augustine's conversion verse.
John.7.6: My time is not yet come; but your time is alway ready. Christ's life followed a divine schedule; ours follows our own. The verse implies messianic timing.
Heb.4.13: All things are naked and opened unto the eyes of Him with whom we have to do. Divine omniscience makes accountability inevitable. Nothing is hidden.
Rev.4.11: Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for thou hast created all things. Creation grounds worthiness of worship. The 24 elders' creed.
Eph.2.2: Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air. Pre-conversion: world-conformed and demon-influenced. The unbeliever's threefold bondage (vv. 1–3): world, flesh, devil.
Matt.20.28: The Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give His life a ransom (lytron) for many. Christ's mission summarized. Substitutionary atonement plainly stated.
Dan.7.13: One like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven... and there was given Him dominion, and glory, and a kingdom. Jesus' favorite self-designation drawn from this verse. Coronation vision.
2Cor.13.5: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Self-examination commanded. Subjective assurance is aided by objective marks.
Matt.19.29: Every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren... for my name's sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life. Discipleship's cost-and-reward economy.
Heb.13.21: Make you perfect (katartisai — equip) in every good work to do His will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in His sight. God both equips and operates within the believer.
Phil.2.3: Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves. The mind of Christ (v. 5) anchors humility-grounded ethics.
1Tim.4.12: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity. Six categories of exemplary Christian conduct.
1Pet.1.14: As obedient children, not fashioning yourselves according to the former lusts in your ignorance. Conversion replaces conformity to ignorance with conformity to the Father.
2Pet.1.3: His divine power hath given unto us all things that pertain unto life and godliness, through the knowledge of Him. Sufficiency of God's resources for sanctified living.
Jas.4.4: Know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God? Friendship-with-the-world is theological hostility. James's rebuke is sharp.
Mark.7.21: Out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications... Thirteen evils named. The heart, not the environment, is the source of moral pollution.
1Chr.29.11: Thine, O LORD, is the greatness, and the power, and the glory, and the victory, and the majesty: for all that is in the heaven and in the earth is thine. David's doxology. Likely the source for the kingdom-power-glory clause in the Lord's Prayer.
Isa.42.1: Behold my servant, whom I uphold; mine elect, in whom my soul delighteth. The first Servant Song. Cited Matt 12:18 of Christ.
Rev.1.8: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the Almighty. Christ's deity asserted by the Father's titles.
Deut.10.12: What doth the LORD thy God require of thee, but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him? Five-fold covenant requirement compressed.
Acts.3.19: Repent ye therefore, and be converted (epistrepsate — turn around), that your sins may be blotted out. Peter's Pentecost-aftermath sermon. Repentance + conversion + forgiveness.
Jas.4.7: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Submission to God enables successful resistance.
Ps.147.3: He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds. Divine pastoral care. The verse anticipates Christ's healing ministry.
1Thess.5.18: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you. Comprehensive thanksgiving as God's revealed will.
Heb.13.15: By Him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually... the fruit of our lips giving thanks to His name. NT priesthood: spoken praise as continual offering.
Col.3.2: Set your affection (phroneite — direct your mind) on things above, not on things on the earth. Mental orientation as the wellspring of behavior.
Jer.9.23: Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom, neither let the mighty man glory in his might. The objects of human boasting are forbidden grounds. Verse 24: only glory in knowing God.
Matt.10.28: Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear Him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell. Fear-prioritization. The fear of God displaces the fear of man.
Isa.35.5: The eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be unstopped. Messianic-age miracles. Cited by Jesus (Matt 11:5) as His own works.
2Tim.4.18: The Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto His heavenly kingdom. Paul's doxology in his last letter. Confidence in eternal preservation despite imminent execution.
Josh.24.15: Choose you this day whom ye will serve... but as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD. Joshua's covenant ultimatum. The household head's spiritual leadership.
Eccl.12.13: Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep His commandments: for this is the whole duty of man. The Preacher's verdict. Reverent obedience is the human telos.
2Thess.3.3: The Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil. Divine faithfulness as the ground of preservation.
Matt.16.27: The Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father with His angels; and then He shall reward every man according to his works. Eschatological judgment with works-based reward (for believers; Rev 20 for unbelievers).
1John.5.4: Whatsoever is born of God overcometh the world: this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith. Faith is the world-overcoming weapon, and regeneration produces it.
Ps.138.8: The LORD will perfect that which concerneth me. Divine completion of personal sanctification. Echoes Phil 1:6.
1Pet.1.7: That the trial (dokimion — testing) of your faith, being much more precious than of gold... might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ. Tested faith's eschatological dividend.
Rom.8.14: As many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God. Spirit-leadership is the indicator of sonship. Test of life.
2Cor.5.15: He died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto Him which died for them and rose again. Christ's death calls for self-displacing life.
Rom.8.26: The Spirit also helpeth (synantilambanetai — joins in to help) our infirmities... the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered. Spirit-prayer beneath our prayers.
1Pet.4.7: The end of all things is at hand: be ye therefore sober, and watch unto prayer. Eschatological proximity calls for prayerful sobriety.
Isa.30.18: The LORD will wait, that He may be gracious unto you... blessed are all they that wait for Him. Even God waits — to gather the qualified recipients of His grace.
Matt.5.44: Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you. Four imperatives toward enemies. The radical Christian ethic.
2Cor.9.6: He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully. Generous giving's harvest principle.
Col.4.2: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving. Three present imperatives — perseverance, vigilance, gratitude — interwoven.
Ps.34.15: The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, and His ears are open unto their cry. Divine attention focused on the righteous. Cited 1 Pet 3:12.
1John.1.8: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us. Self-perception of sinlessness is self-deception. Confessional Christianity vs. perfectionist illusion.
Matt.6.9: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. The Lord's Prayer opens with relationship and reverence. Father (immanence) and heaven (transcendence) balanced.
Rom.8.35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress... Seven catastrophic candidates listed. Verse 37: in all these things we are more than conquerors.
Ps.29.11: The LORD will give strength unto His people; the LORD will bless His people with peace. Divine bestowal of strength and peace as covenantal benediction.
Titus.3.3: We ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived... Pre-conversion catalog. The 'we' includes the apostle. Salvation's ground (v. 4–5) is mercy, not merit.
Prov.28.26: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered. Self-trust is foolishness. Cited inferentially throughout the Reformed tradition's rejection of carnal reason.
1Cor.1.9: God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the fellowship (koinōnia) of His Son Jesus Christ. Calling is into relationship. Divine faithfulness undergirds calling's permanence.
Eph.4.1: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation (klēsis) wherewith ye are called. Calling generates corresponding ethics.
Ps.30.5: Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning. Sorrow's brevity vs. joy's morning arrival. The pattern of divine consolation.
1Chr.4.10: Jabez called on the God of Israel, saying, Oh that thou wouldest bless me indeed, and enlarge my coast. The unexpected prayer in the genealogy. God granted his request.
Acts.20.24: None of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry. Paul's vocational priorities. Mission outranks self-preservation.
Prov.10.22: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and He addeth no sorrow with it. Divine prosperity comes without divine grief. Riches by other means do not.
1Tim.1.15: This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief. Paul's gospel summary with self-identification as chief sinner.
Luke.21.36: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things... and to stand before the Son of man. Eschatological vigilance.
2Cor.5.7: We walk by faith, not by sight. Faith vs. sight as competing modes of orientation. Christian living suspends physical evaluation for spiritual reality.
Eph.1.18: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of His calling, and what the riches of the glory of His inheritance. Spiritual perception as the result of divine illumination.
Rev.11.15: The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of His Christ; and He shall reign for ever and ever. Eschatological transfer. The seventh trumpet announcement.
John.6.45: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me. Divine pedagogy precedes coming to Christ.
Ps.119.1: Blessed are the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the LORD. The longest psalm opens with a beatitude on Torah-walking. The whole psalm celebrates the Word.
Zech.12.10: They shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for Him. Future Israel's recognition of the crucified Messiah. Cited John 19:37 of the cross; ultimate fulfillment at Christ's return.
Gal.5.19: The works of the flesh are manifest, which are these: adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness... Fifteen vices listed (vv. 19–21). Compare with the fruit of the Spirit (v. 22).
Gal.5.24: They that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts. Past-tense decisive crucifixion of flesh as the believer's positional reality.
Ps.16.11: In thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore. Divine presence is the supreme good. Cited Acts 2:28 as fulfilled in Christ's resurrection.
Gen.1.26: Let us make man in our image, after our likeness — divine plurality (Trinitarian foreshadowing). Image-bearing grounds human dignity and dominion. The 'us' problematizes simple unitarianism.
Rev.22.17: The Spirit and the bride say, Come... whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely. The Bible closes with a free invitation. Spirit, church, and hearers join the call.
1Cor.2.14: The natural man (psychikos) receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God; for they are foolishness unto him. Spiritual things require spiritual perception. Apologetics has its limit at regeneration.
Jer.23.5: I will raise unto David a righteous Branch (tsemach), and a King shall reign and prosper. Messianic prophecy. The Branch motif (Isa 11:1, Zech 3:8) coalesces in Christ.
John.15.2: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit He taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, He purgeth (kathairei — prunes) it. Divine pruning of fruitful branches. Discipline serves productivity.
1Pet.2.12: Having your conversation honest among the Gentiles: that... by your good works, which they shall behold, glorify God. Visible Christian conduct as evangelistic apologetic.
Phil.4.4: Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice. Doubled imperative for emphasis. Joy is commanded — and located in the Lord, not in circumstance.
Rom.8.9: If any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of His. Possession of the Spirit is the test of Christ-belonging. No Spirit, no salvation.
Heb.6.18: By two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation. The two: God's promise and His oath (Gen 22:16–17). Double divine bond.
John.16.13: He, the Spirit of truth, is come, He will guide you into all truth: for He shall not speak of Himself. The Spirit's apostolic guidance, undergirding the New Testament's inspiration.
Matt.16.18: Upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it. The rock is Peter's confession (v. 16) — Christ Himself. The church's invincibility guaranteed.
1Pet.1.2: Elect according to the foreknowledge (prognōsin) of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit, unto obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ. Trinitarian salvation. Election precedes sanctification precedes obedience.
1Pet.4.8: Above all things have fervent charity (ektenē — stretched-out love) among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins. Love's covering function — overlooking offenses.
Eccl.9.10: Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might; for there is no work, nor device... in the grave. Wholehearted labor in the brief window of life.
Matt.5.11: Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake. The eighth beatitude. Persecution-for-righteousness is blessed.
1Pet.1.8: Whom having not seen, ye love; in whom, though now ye see Him not, yet believing, ye rejoice with joy unspeakable. Faith without sight produces love and joy. The verse undergirds Christian assurance for those who never met Christ in the flesh.
Isa.1.18: Come now, and let us reason together: though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow. The covenant lawsuit pivots to grace. Divine forensic invitation grounded in cleansing.
Deut.32.30: How should one chase a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight, except their Rock had sold them? The Song of Moses on covenant cause-and-effect. Divine forsakenness explains military rout.
1Pet.1.13: Gird up the loins of your mind (anazōsamenoi tas osphyas tēs dianoias), be sober, and hope to the end. Mental discipline is precondition for hope. The metaphor: tucking up robes for action.
Matt.16.24: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me. Three present imperatives — daily, not once. Discipleship is sustained self-renunciation.
Ezek.34.13: I will bring them out from the people, and gather them from the countries, and feed them upon the mountains of Israel. Eschatological regathering and pastoral care promised after Israel's faithless shepherds (vv. 1–10).
Phil.3.9: Found in Him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ. Imputed righteousness contrasted with self-righteousness. Justification's mechanic stated.
Gal.6.1: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore (katartizete — set the broken bone) such an one in the spirit of meekness. Restorative discipline, not punitive shaming.
Heb.9.14: How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered Himself without spot to God, purge your conscience? Trinitarian atonement. Christ's blood, applied by the Spirit, cleanses the conscience.
Matt.1.21: Thou shalt call His name JESUS: for He shall save His people from their sins. The angel's announcement. Jesus (Yēshua, 'Yahweh saves') prophesies the function of the person.
John.16.23: Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, He will give it you. Prayer in Christ's name = prayer aligned with His mediation and character. Not a magic formula but a relational reality.
Phil.2.12: Work out (katergazesthe — bring to completion) your own salvation with fear and trembling. Sanctification follows salvation, not earns it. Verse 13 grounds it: God works in you.
Rom.3.23: All have sinned, and come short of the glory of God. Universal indictment in two clauses. Hamartanō (sinned) is missing-the-mark; the glory of God is the standard.
Isa.40.11: He shall feed His flock like a shepherd: He shall gather the lambs with His arm, and carry them in His bosom. Pastoral imagery anticipating Christ the Good Shepherd. Tender particularity.
1Pet.3.9: Not rendering evil for evil, or railing for railing; but contrariwise blessing. Christian non-retaliation. Knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a blessing.
Matt.6.13: Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil (or 'the evil one'). The closing petition of the Lord's Prayer. Acknowledges human weakness and demonic adversity in one breath.
1John.4.1: Believe not every spirit, but try (dokimazete — test) the spirits whether they are of God. Many false prophets are gone out into the world. Discernment is a Christian duty.
Jas.4.6: He giveth more grace. Wherefore He saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble. Cited from Prov 3:34. Grace-distribution along the axis of humility.
Rom.10.17: Faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God. Saving faith requires the gospel preached. The means of grace.
Eph.4.24: Put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness. The change-of-clothes metaphor. The new man is divinely created in moral excellence.
Ps.145.19: He will fulfil the desire of them that fear Him: He also will hear their cry, and will save them. Reverent fear is the precondition; divine response is fourfold.
Jer.23.3: I will gather the remnant of my flock out of all countries whither I have driven them. Despite faithless shepherds, God Himself will regather. Eschatological promise.
2Cor.4.18: While we look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. Things seen are temporal; things unseen are eternal. Christian sight is faith-mediated.
Gal.3.28: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus. Equality of standing in Christ. Does not erase ontological distinctions but unites in salvation.
Prov.28.13: He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy. Confession and forsaking — twin movements of repentance.
Heb.10.25: Not forsaking the assembling (episynagōgēn) of ourselves together, as the manner of some is. Corporate worship is non-negotiable. Exhorting one another, and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.
Isa.58.11: The LORD shall guide thee continually, and satisfy thy soul in drought. Conditional on the social-justice ethic of vv. 6–10. Personal blessing flows through covenantal righteousness.
Matt.5.5: Blessed are the meek (praeis): for they shall inherit the earth. Cited from Ps 37:11. The world goes to the strong; the new earth goes to the meek.
Phil.3.8: I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord. Counting (hēgoumai) — present-tense reckoning. All earthly credentials are dung (skybala) compared to Christ.
1Pet.3.8: Be ye all of one mind (homophrones), having compassion one of another, love as brethren, be pitiful, be courteous. Five marks of Christian community.
2Tim.4.7: I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith. Paul's deathbed verdict. Three perfect tenses — accomplished and abiding.
Ps.28.7: The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in Him, and I am helped. The pattern of OT trust-and-deliverance.
1Cor.15.33: Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners. Cited from Menander, the Greek poet. Companions shape character.
1John.2.27: The anointing which ye have received of Him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man teach you. Spirit-given understanding. Not an anti-teacher polemic but assurance against false-teacher seduction.
Deut.4.29: If from thence thou shalt seek the LORD thy God, thou shalt find Him, if thou seek Him with all thy heart and with all thy soul. Wholehearted seeking-from-exile is rewarded.
Heb.5.7: Who in the days of His flesh, when He had offered up prayers and supplications with strong crying and tears. Christ's Gethsemane prayer. Was heard in that He feared (eulabeia, reverence).
1Cor.1.10: That ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions (schismata) among you. Doctrinal unity, ecclesiastical wholeness. Paul's first appeal to a fractured Corinth.
Col.3.13: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another... even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye. Christological forgiveness as the standard.
Gen.50.20: Ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good. Joseph's confession. Compatibilism in narrative form: human evil and divine purpose simultaneously real.
Heb.13.16: To do good and to communicate (koinōnias — sharing) forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased. Doing-good and giving as priestly sacrifices.
John.8.44: Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. Jesus' starkest indictment of unbelieving religious leaders. Spiritual paternity manifests in moral character.
Ps.27.13: I had fainted, unless I had believed to see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living. Faith for present-life vindication, not just eschatological.
Mic.7.7: I will look unto the LORD; I will wait for the God of my salvation: my God will hear me. Threefold confession in despair. Faith resolves to wait.
John.10.9: I am the door (thyra): by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved. The seventh I AM saying. Christ as the singular point of access.
Jude.1.20: Building up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Ghost. Self-edification through doctrine and Spirit-prayer. Antidote to apostasy.
Rev.1.7: Behold, He cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see Him, and they also which pierced Him. Public, universal, vindicating return. Cited from Dan 7 and Zech 12.
Rom.6.4: We are buried with Him by baptism into death... we also should walk in newness of life. Baptism's symbolism: union with Christ's death and resurrection.
Ps.46.7: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. The refrain of Psalm 46 — the basis for Luther's 'A Mighty Fortress.'
Isa.40.28: The everlasting God, the LORD, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary. Divine inexhaustibility — God doesn't tire.
Ps.86.15: Thou, O Lord, art a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth. Cites Exod 34:6. The OT's most-cited self-revelation.
2Chr.16.9: The eyes of the LORD run to and fro throughout the whole earth, to shew Himself strong in the behalf of them whose heart is perfect (shalem) toward Him. God scouts for whole-hearted devotees to bless.
Heb.10.16: This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts. Cites Jer 31. The new covenant inaugurated.
John.12.46: I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness. Christ as light counters cosmic spiritual darkness.
1Pet.1.20: Foreordained (proegnōsmenou) before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you. Christ's incarnation pre-temporally planned.
Luke.7.50: Thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace. The sinful woman who washed Jesus' feet. Faith — not works — saved her, despite her great sin.
Ps.3.3: But thou, O LORD, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head. Three covenantal images. David's response to Absalom's rebellion.
Phil.2.9: God also hath highly exalted Him, and given Him a name which is above every name. The exaltation following the kenosis (vv. 6–8). Cosmic enthronement.
Hos.4.6: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge: because thou hast rejected knowledge. Ignorance of God's law leads to ruin.
Ps.133.1: Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity! The Song of Ascents on covenant fellowship.
Ps.34.9: O fear the LORD, ye His saints: for there is no want to them that fear Him. Reverent fear matched with covenantal supply.
1John.1.1: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes... declare we unto you. Apostolic eyewitness as the foundation of Christian testimony.
Mic.5.2: Thou, Bethlehem Ephratah... out of thee shall He come forth unto me that is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings forth have been from of old, from everlasting. Pre-existent Messiah's earthly birthplace foretold.
Ps.23.3: He restoreth my soul: He leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for His name's sake. Soul-restoration and ethical guidance — divine, for divine glory.
Prov.13.20: He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed. Companionship shapes destiny.
Matt.5.4: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted. The second beatitude. Mourning over sin attracts divine consolation.
Isa.49.25: I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children. Divine partisanship for the covenant people.
2Pet.1.5: Add to your faith virtue; and to virtue knowledge... Seven graces piled (vv. 5–7). Diligent supplementation of faith.
Ps.1.2: His delight is in the law of the LORD; and in His law doth he meditate (yehgeh — murmur, ponder) day and night. The blessed man's relationship to Scripture.
Heb.10.24: Let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works. Mutual stimulation toward Christian virtue.
Ps.18.2: The LORD is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust. Eight-fold metaphor cluster. David's confession.
Phil.2.6: Who, being in the form (morphē) of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God. Christ's pre-existent deity. Verses 6–11 form the great Christ-hymn.
Ps.14.3: They are all gone aside, they are all together become filthy: there is none that doeth good, no, not one. Cited Rom 3:12. Total depravity stated unflinchingly.
Col.3.15: Let the peace of God rule (brabeuetō — umpire) in your hearts. Peace as the arbiter of inner life and church relations.
Col.1.23: If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled, and be not moved away from the hope of the gospel. Continuance is the proof of genuine conversion.
1Pet.4.2: That he no longer should live the rest of his time in the flesh to the lusts of men, but to the will of God. Conversion is volitional reorientation.
1Pet.1.15: As He which hath called you is holy, so be ye holy in all manner of conversation (anastrophē — conduct). Imitation of God in moral character.
Isa.60.19: The LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light, and thy God thy glory. Eschatological consummation — God Himself replaces sun and moon.
1Pet.1.18: Ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold... but with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish. Substitutionary atonement at infinite cost.
Matt.6.10: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven. The petition of kingdom-aligned desire.
Ps.119.9: Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? By taking heed thereto according to thy word. The Word as moral cleansing for the young.
Ps.27.5: In the time of trouble He shall hide me in His pavilion: in the secret of His tabernacle shall He hide me. Sanctuary imagery — God's presence is the hiding place.
Exod.20.12: Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long. The fifth commandment, the first with promise (Eph 6:2).
1Cor.4.5: Judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts. Reserve final verdicts to Christ's tribunal.
Heb.10.23: Let us hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering; for He is faithful that promised. Christian perseverance grounded in divine faithfulness.
Rom.11.33: O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable are His judgments. Doxological response to chapters 9–11. God's plan exceeds investigation.
2Chr.20.17: Ye shall not need to fight in this battle: set yourselves, stand ye still, and see the salvation of the LORD. Jehoshaphat's victory by passive faith.
Acts.14.22: We must through much tribulation enter into the kingdom of God. Suffering is the road, not the obstacle.
1Pet.3.15: Sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give an answer (apologia) to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope. Apologetics commanded — gentle and reverent.
Isa.54.13: All thy children shall be taught of the LORD; and great shall be the peace of thy children. Eschatological covenant family — divinely instructed.
Rom.8.37: In all these things we are more than conquerors (hypernikōmen) through Him that loved us. Super-conquerors through Christ — not in spite of suffering but in it.
1Cor.9.24: They which run in a race run all, but one receiveth the prize? So run, that ye may obtain. Christian life as athletic discipline.
Rev.15.4: Who shall not fear thee, O Lord, and glorify thy name? for thou only art holy. The song of Moses and the Lamb. Universal eschatological worship.
Ps.23.5: Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies: thou anointest my head with oil. Covenant table set in hostile territory.
Matt.5.9: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God. Peacemaking marks divine sonship.
Heb.9.28: Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for Him shall He appear the second time without sin unto salvation. Two appearings. The second is for consummated salvation.
2Thess.2.16: Now our Lord Jesus Christ Himself, and God, even our Father, which hath loved us, and hath given us everlasting consolation and good hope through grace. Trinitarian benediction.
Jas.1.21: Receive with meekness the engrafted (emphyton) word, which is able to save your souls. Implanted Word — the gospel within.
Ps.125.1: They that trust in the LORD shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever. Settled stability for the trusting.
Rom.15.4: Whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope. The OT's purpose for NT believers.
Isa.40.8: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever. Cited 1 Pet 1:24–25. Divine durability vs. human transience.
Ps.37.23: The steps of a good man are ordered (kun — established) by the LORD: and He delighteth in his way. Providential micromanagement of the righteous.
Luke.24.47: Repentance and remission of sins should be preached in His name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem. Christ's Great Commission in Luke's framing.
Phil.3.10: That I may know Him, and the power of His resurrection, and the fellowship of His sufferings. Paul's ultimate ambition. Resurrection-power and cross-fellowship inseparable.
Ps.36.8: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou shalt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures. Divine extravagance toward His people.
Isa.35.8: An highway shall be there, and a way, and it shall be called The way of holiness. Eschatological pilgrimage road — accessible to the redeemed.
Ps.143.8: Cause me to hear thy lovingkindness in the morning; for in thee do I trust. Daily-morning request for renewed assurance.
Isa.11.2: The Spirit of the LORD shall rest upon Him: the spirit of wisdom and understanding... Sevenfold Spirit-anointing on the Messianic Branch.
1Tim.6.12: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called. The Christian life as deliberate combat.
Rom.8.13: If ye through the Spirit do mortify (thanatoute — kill) the deeds of the body, ye shall live. Sanctification by Spirit-empowered killing of sin.
Isa.64.4: Since the beginning of the world men have not heard, nor perceived... what He hath prepared for him that waiteth for Him. Cited 1 Cor 2:9.
Isa.29.13: This people draw near me with their mouth... but have removed their heart far from me. Cited by Jesus (Matt 15:8) of Pharisees.
John.14.23: If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him. Trinitarian indwelling conditioned on loving obedience.
Gal.4.4: When the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth His Son, made of a woman, made under the law. Incarnation in Pauline compression.
Isa.8.10: Take counsel together, and it shall come to nought; speak the word, and it shall not stand: for God is with us. Anti-imperial confidence: Immanuel principle.
Rom.8.30: Whom He did predestinate, them He also called: and whom He called, them He also justified: and whom He justified, them He also glorified. The unbreakable golden chain of salvation.
Prov.13.3: He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction. Mouth-discipline as life-preservation.
Prov.21.30: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the LORD. Human strategy cannot frustrate divine purpose.
Matt.3.2: Repent ye: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand. John the Baptist's compressed message — and Jesus' opening word (Matt 4:17).
Ps.19.14: Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight, O LORD. The standard prayer for speech and thought purity.
Heb.1.2: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by His Son... by whom also He made the worlds. Christ as both final revelation and creator of the ages.
Rev.12.9: The great dragon was cast out, that old serpent... which deceiveth the whole world. Satan identified across multiple titles. The dragon's defeat.
Prov.28.1: The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion. Guilt-induced paranoia vs. covenantal courage.
John.10.30: I and my Father are one (hen). Jesus' assertion of essential unity with the Father. The Jews respond by attempting to stone Him for blasphemy.
Eph.2.18: Through Him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father. Trinitarian access — the result of cross-reconciliation between Jew and Gentile.
Rom.2.7: To them who by patient continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and immortality, eternal life. Reward-language for genuine faith demonstrated by perseverance.
1Thess.5.11: Comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do. Mutual encouragement is the church's sustained practice.
Jer.24.7: I will give them an heart to know me, that I am the LORD. Regeneration produces relational knowledge of God.
Prov.21.3: To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice. Ethics over ritual — the prophetic refrain.
Deut.16.16: Three times in a year shall all thy males appear before the LORD thy God in the place which He shall choose. The pilgrimage festivals (Passover, Weeks, Tabernacles).
Matt.15.19: Out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications... The catalog of heart-products. Defilement is internal-out, not external-in.
Deut.7.9: The LORD thy God, He is God, the faithful God, which keepeth covenant and mercy with them that love Him to a thousand generations. Covenantal fidelity across millennia.
Ps.103.3: Who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases. Twin divine acts. Forgiveness includes spiritual healing.
Rev.3.19: As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten: be zealous therefore, and repent. Divine love expressed through correction.
Ps.63.1: O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee. David's wilderness longing for God.
2Thess.2.13: God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth. Election unto sanctification, applied through Spirit and faith.
1Cor.6.11: Such were some of you: but ye are washed, but ye are sanctified, but ye are justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and by the Spirit of our God. Triple aorist transformation.
Isa.61.10: He hath clothed me with the garments of salvation, He hath covered me with the robe of righteousness. Imputed-righteousness imagery.
1John.3.16: Hereby perceive we the love of God, because He laid down His life for us: and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren. Love defined and demanded by the cross.
Phil.2.15: That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation. Christian distinctiveness in moral darkness.
1John.2.3: Hereby we do know that we know Him, if we keep His commandments. Obedience as test of genuine knowledge.
2Pet.3.18: Grow in grace, and in the knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. Sanctification as growth — relational and intellectual.
Deut.30.6: The LORD thy God will circumcise thine heart, and the heart of thy seed, to love the LORD thy God with all thine heart. Heart-circumcision — anticipating new covenant.
Isa.49.6: I will also give thee for a light to the Gentiles, that thou mayest be my salvation unto the end of the earth. The Servant's mission to nations. Cited Acts 13:47.
1Pet.5.6: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that He may exalt you in due time. Self-humbling under sovereign timing.
Prov.3.1: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments. The fatherly preface to the Solomonic instructions.
John.8.42: If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God. Loving the Son is the test of having the Father.
Rom.13.11: It is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed. Eschatological vigilance.
John.3.17: God sent not His Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through Him might be saved. The first advent's purpose. Judgment is incidental to mission.
Ps.34.19: Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all. Suffering pluralized; deliverance comprehensive.
Isa.61.3: To give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness. The Servant's reversals for the broken-hearted.
Gen.17.1: I am the Almighty God (El Shaddai); walk before me, and be thou perfect (tamim — whole). Covenant name plus covenant call.
Matt.25.40: Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me. Christ's identification with His persecuted people.
Isa.41.13: I the LORD thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not. Personal divine grasp dispels fear.
Matt.5.10: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. The eighth beatitude. Persecution is kingdom-credential.
Isa.3.10: Say ye to the righteous, that it shall be well with him: for they shall eat the fruit of their doings. Cause-effect for the righteous.
2Thess.3.16: Now the Lord of peace Himself give you peace always by all means. Doxological wish — peace from the Lord, in every mode.
Matt.7.15: Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves. The deception of false teachers.
Rom.8.6: To be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace. Two mindsets, two destinies.
Rom.12.9: Let love be without dissimulation (anypokritos — un-hypocritical). Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good. Genuine love hates evil.
Eph.5.26: That He might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word. Christ's sanctifying work on the church through Word-mediated cleansing.
Gal.5.6: In Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love. Faith expressed in love is the only valid currency.
John.10.14: I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine. Mutual knowledge between shepherd and sheep — a relational soteriology.
Col.3.3: Ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God. The believer's life is concealed in trinitarian protection until Christ's appearing.
1Cor.10.31: Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do all to the glory of God. The Christian totalism. No neutral acts.
Isa.53.6: All we like sheep have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way; and the LORD hath laid on Him the iniquity of us all. Substitutionary atonement in seven Hebrew words.
Luke.6.38: Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over. Generosity's reciprocal economy.
1Sam.30.6: David encouraged himself in the LORD his God. Self-encouragement by fixing on covenantal truth, not feelings.
John.5.39: Search the scriptures... they are they which testify of me. Christ as the OT's true subject.
Rom.5.5: Hope maketh not ashamed; because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost. Inner Spirit-witness as the basis of unshameable hope.
Phil.3.20: Our conversation (politeuma — citizenship) is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour. Heavenly citizenship awaiting eschatological consummation.
2Chr.15.7: Be ye strong therefore, and let not your hands be weak: for your work shall be rewarded. Reformation under Asa: persistence rewarded.
Eph.1.6: To the praise of the glory of His grace, wherein He hath made us accepted (echaritōsen — graced) in the beloved. Election's terminus is doxology.
Heb.10.35: Cast not away therefore your confidence (parrēsia), which hath great recompence of reward. Christian boldness has eschatological dividend.
Isa.44.22: I have blotted out, as a thick cloud, thy transgressions, and, as a cloud, thy sins: return unto me; for I have redeemed thee. Forgiveness is the ground of repentance, not its result.
Luke.9.23: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me. The 'daily' is unique to Luke. Discipleship is sustained, not single-event.
Rev.3.21: To him that overcometh will I grant to sit with me in my throne, even as I also overcame. Co-enthronement with Christ for perseverers.
Rev.20.12: I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened. The Great White Throne. Universal accountability.
Matt.11.25: I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes. Sovereign election in revelation.
Jer.1.9: Then the LORD put forth His hand, and touched my mouth. And the LORD said... I have put my words in thy mouth. Prophetic commissioning by direct divine action.
Rev.22.14: Blessed are they that do His commandments, that they may have right to the tree of life. Eschatological access conditioned on obedience-rooted-in-faith.
Heb.2.14: That through death He might destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the devil. Christ's death-defeat-by-death paradox.
Lam.3.26: It is good that a man should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the LORD. Quiet hope amid devastation.
Luke.23.34: Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do. Christ's first cross-word. Intercession at the moment of greatest injustice.
Rom.5.6: When we were yet without strength, in due time Christ died for the ungodly. Substitution at the right moment for the helpless.
John.17.21: That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us. Trinitarian unity as the model and means of church unity.
Col.1.9: That ye might be filled with the knowledge (epignōsis) of His will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding. Comprehensive divine illumination as Paul's prayer.
Gal.6.10: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith. Universal beneficence with covenantal priority.
John.8.31: If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed. Genuineness measured by continuance.
1Thess.5.24: Faithful is He that calleth you, who also will do it. Divine faithfulness underwrites complete sanctification (v. 23).
John.14.16: He shall give you another Comforter (paraklētos — alongside-helper), that He may abide with you for ever. The Spirit's permanent indwelling, distinct from yet equal to Christ.
Prov.1.7: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction. Epistemology's foundation. The book's thesis.
1John.4.15: Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God. Christological confession as the seal of divine indwelling.
Matt.16.16: Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God. Peter's great confession. The bedrock of the church.
Isa.42.5: Thus saith God the LORD, He that created the heavens... He that giveth breath unto the people upon it. Creator-credential before Servant-commission.
Job.28.28: The fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding. Wisdom's nature defined.
1John.2.1: My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. And if any man sin, we have an advocate (paraklēton) with the Father, Jesus Christ. Advocacy in heaven for the failing believer.
Jas.1.27: Pure religion (thrēskeia) and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction. Religion's twin manifestations: care for the vulnerable, separation from the world.
John.3.18: He that believeth on Him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already. Verdict already rendered; faith is the crucial act.
Rom.16.20: The God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly. Echoes Gen 3:15. Christ's victory now extends to the church.
Isa.25.9: Lo, this is our God; we have waited for Him, and He will save us. The eschatological exultation of the saved.
Rom.12.10: Be kindly affectioned (philostorgoi) one to another with brotherly love (philadelphia); in honour preferring one another. Family-like ecclesial ethic.
2Pet.1.19: We have also a more sure word of prophecy; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed. Scripture as more reliable than even Peter's mountaintop experience.
Deut.31.8: The LORD, He it is that doth go before thee; He will be with thee, He will not fail thee, neither forsake thee. Covenantal reassurance to Joshua.
1Tim.6.17: Charge them that are rich in this world... that they trust not in uncertain riches, but in the living God. Pastoral counsel to wealthy believers.
1Pet.2.11: Abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul. Christian sojourners must resist the indwelling adversary of the flesh.
1Tim.4.1: In the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits. Apostasy predicted. Spirit-prophesied.
Rev.22.3: There shall be no more curse: but the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it. The reversal of Eden's curse in the new creation.
Matt.6.19: Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth... but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven. Investment-portfolio metaphor for eternal stewardship.
1Thess.2.13: Ye received it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God. Apostolic preaching equaled with divine speech.
1John.3.8: For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that He might destroy the works of the devil. Christ's mission's anti-demonic dimension.
1Pet.1.19: With the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot. Sacrificial-lamb typology applied to Christ's redeeming blood.
Rev.3.8: I have set before thee an open door, and no man can shut it. Christ's promise to faithful Philadelphia. Sovereign opening of mission-doors.
2Pet.3.10: The day of the Lord will come as a thief in the night; in the which the heavens shall pass away with a great noise. Cosmic dissolution at the eschaton.
Mic.6.1: Hear ye now what the LORD saith; Arise, contend thou before the mountains. Yahweh's covenant lawsuit against Israel.
Phil.1.27: Let your conversation (politeuesthe — live as citizens) be as it becometh the gospel of Christ. Christian citizenship-of-the-gospel.
Acts.2.39: The promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, even as many as the Lord our God shall call. Pentecost's promise universalized but particularized by divine calling.
Amos.3.7: Surely the Lord GOD will do nothing, but He revealeth His secret unto His servants the prophets. Prophetic mediation of divine purpose.
Deut.11.27: A blessing, if ye obey the commandments of the LORD your God. Moses sets blessing/curse before Israel.
1John.1.5: God is light, and in Him is no darkness at all. Divine moral purity stated absolutely.
Isa.25.8: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord GOD will wipe away tears from off all faces. Cited 1 Cor 15:54. Death's defeat at the eschaton.
Lev.19.18: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD. The second great commandment, cited by Jesus.
Heb.10.38: The just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him. Cites Hab 2:4. Faith as the just life-principle.
1Pet.3.4: The hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit. Inner adornment over external.
Hos.10.12: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap in mercy; break up your fallow ground: for it is time to seek the LORD. Agricultural metaphor for repentance.
Matt.5.7: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy. Reciprocal mercy economy in the kingdom.
Eph.6.13: Take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day. Comprehensive divine outfitting for the eschatological conflict.
Jer.32.38: They shall be my people, and I will be their God. The covenant formula throughout Scripture.
Isa.9.2: The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light. Cited Matt 4:16 of Christ's Galilean ministry.
Heb.4.15: We have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin. Christ's full humanity except for sin.
Prov.19.17: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD; and that which he hath given will He pay him again. Generosity to the poor as loan-to-God.
Luke.6.35: Love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest. Heavenly reward for un-reciprocated love.
Lam.3.22: It is of the LORD's mercies (chesed) that we are not consumed, because His compassions fail not. Mercy as preservation amid judgment.
Heb.1.14: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation? Angels' role: serving the elect.
John.6.37: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out. Particular grace + universal welcome.
Titus.1.16: They profess that they know God; but in works they deny Him. False profession exposed by works.
Rom.10.11: Whosoever believeth on Him shall not be ashamed. Cited from Isa 28:16. Faith vindicated.
Rom.13.9: Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not kill... if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying, namely, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. Love sums the second table.
Titus.3.4: After that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man (philanthrōpia) appeared. The incarnation as divine philanthropy.
Luke.14.26: If any man come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife... he cannot be my disciple. Hate as comparative — radical priority of Christ.
Rom.7.22: I delight in the law of God after the inward man. The regenerate self loves God's law even amid struggle.
Jer.31.34: They shall all know me, from the least of them unto the greatest of them... I will forgive their iniquity. New covenant's universal knowledge and forgiveness.
John.12.40: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart. Cites Isa 6:10. Divine judicial hardening.
Ps.51.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise. The acceptable sacrifice is interior brokenness.
2Cor.8.9: He was rich, yet for your sakes He became poor, that ye through His poverty might be rich. The great exchange in giving.
Matt.5.14: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid. Christian visibility — non-optional.
Gen.17.7: I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee in their generations for an everlasting covenant. The Abrahamic covenant's perpetuity.
1John.4.7: Beloved, let us love one another: for love is of God; and every one that loveth is born of God. Love as evidence of regeneration.
Rom.5.2: By whom also we have access by faith into this grace wherein we stand, and rejoice in hope of the glory of God. Standing-in-grace as the locus of Christian joy.
Job.5.12: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise. Divine frustration of human scheming.
Isa.42.6: I the LORD have called thee in righteousness, and will hold thine hand, and will keep thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles. Servant-of-Yahweh's commission.
Matt.25.21: Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things... enter thou into the joy of thy lord. Master's verdict on faithful stewardship.
Job.23.13: He is in one mind, and who can turn Him? and what His soul desireth, even that He doeth. Divine immutability and sovereignty.
Rom.12.16: Be of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but condescend to men of low estate. Humility-grounded ecclesial unity.
Ps.1.6: The LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish. Two paths, two destinies — the Psalter's framing verse.
Dan.2.44: In the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed. Eschatological kingdom anticipated in Daniel's interpretation.
Eph.4.2: With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love. Four virtues for unity-keeping.
Job.42.2: I know that thou canst do every thing, and that no thought can be withholden from thee. Job's final confession — divine omnipotence acknowledged.
1Cor.1.18: The preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the power of God. Two opposite responses to the same message.
1Thess.4.3: This is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication. God's revealed will: sanctification, particularly sexual purity.
Jer.33.6: I will bring it health and cure, and I will cure them, and will reveal unto them the abundance of peace and truth. Eschatological restoration of Judah.
Deut.8.3: Man doth not live by bread only, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of the LORD doth man live. Cited by Jesus against Satan (Matt 4:4).
Isa.51.6: The heavens shall vanish away like smoke, and the earth shall wax old like a garment... but my salvation shall be for ever. Cosmic transience vs. salvation's permanence.
Gal.6.2: Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ. Mutual burden-bearing as Christ's law.
2Tim.1.12: I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that He is able to keep that which I have committed unto Him against that day. Personal assurance grounded in divine ability.
John.1.3: All things were made by Him; and without Him was not any thing made that was made. Christ as agent of creation. Verse rules out any uncreated thing besides God.
Isa.53.4: Surely He hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows. Cited Matt 8:17 of Christ's healing ministry. Foundational Servant-suffering.
Heb.13.17: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves... they watch for your souls. Pastoral authority and the duty of congregational submission.
Rom.1.28: Even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind. Judicial abandonment to depravity.
Isa.8.17: I will wait upon the LORD, that hideth His face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for Him. Faith persists when God seems hidden.
Titus.2.13: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ. Granville-Sharp construction asserting Christ's deity.
Isa.58.8: Then shall thy light break forth as the morning, and thine health shall spring forth speedily. Promised effects of true fasting/righteousness.
Prov.2.1: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee. The conditional structure of wisdom's reception.
Acts.20.35: Remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how He said, It is more blessed to give than to receive. Christ's saying preserved by Paul, not in the Gospels.
Deut.29.29: The secret things belong unto the LORD our God: but those things which are revealed belong unto us. Epistemological humility — God's hidden will vs. revealed Word.
Rom.2.4: Despisest thou the riches of His goodness... not knowing that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance. Divine kindness as the engine of repentance.
1Pet.1.23: Born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever. Regeneration through the imperishable Word.
Gal.1.10: Do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ. Audience-orientation determines servanthood.
Phil.1.9: That your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all judgment. Love disciplined by discernment.
Luke.12.31: Seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added unto you. Kingdom-priority brings provisional supply.
Col.3.11: There is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision... but Christ is all, and in all. Christ-centric soteriology that flattens ethnic and social distinctions.
John.15.1: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman. The seventh I AM saying. Christ as covenantally-true Israel.
Lam.3.40: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the LORD. Self-examination leading to repentance.
Deut.32.39: See now that I, even I, am He, and there is no god with me: I kill, and I make alive; I wound, and I heal. The double assertion of monotheism plus divine sovereignty.
Ps.36.7: How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of thy wings. Refuge imagery — chesed under divine wings.
Zech.13.7: Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, and against the man that is my fellow... smite the shepherd. Cited Matt 26:31 of Christ's arrest. Messianic prophecy.
Ps.24.1: The earth is the LORD's, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein. Universal divine ownership.
Eph.5.11: Have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them. Disengagement plus exposure of darkness.
Rom.15.1: We then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves. Strength's responsibility toward weakness.
Rev.15.3: Great and marvellous are thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true are thy ways. The song of Moses and the Lamb.
2Chr.15.2: The LORD is with you, while ye be with Him; and if ye seek Him, He will be found of you. The conditional principle of covenant communion.
2Tim.3.15: From a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith. Childhood Bible-instruction as soteric foundation.
Dan.2.21: He changeth the times and the seasons: He removeth kings, and setteth up kings. Divine sovereignty over geopolitics.
Rom.1.17: The just shall live by faith. Cites Hab 2:4. The Reformation watchword.
Jas.1.26: If any man among you seem to be religious, and bridleth not his tongue... this man's religion is vain. Tongue-control as religion's authenticity test.
Ps.32.7: Thou art my hiding place; thou shalt preserve me from trouble; thou shalt compass me about with songs of deliverance. God as surrounding shelter.
Isa.51.12: I, even I, am He that comforteth you: who art thou, that thou shouldest be afraid of a man? Divine comfort dispelling fear of mortal threat.
Eph.1.13: In whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise. Spirit-sealing as the believer's guarantee of redemption.
Hos.6.1: Come, and let us return unto the LORD: for He hath torn, and He will heal us. Repentance to the chastening God.
1Tim.2.4: Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth. Divine salvific desire — the offer is genuinely universal.
John.4.34: My meat is to do the will of Him that sent me, and to finish His work. Christ's sustenance is mission-completion.
Luke.21.34: Take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life. Eschatological vigilance.
Eph.4.11: He gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers. Christ's ascension-gifts to the church.
Gen.12.2: I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing. The Abrahamic call — universal blessing through one chosen line.
2Tim.2.22: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace. Flight from sin paired with pursuit of virtue.
Eph.4.26: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath. Cites Ps 4:4. Anger as a real but bounded emotion.
Lev.26.8: Five of you shall chase an hundred, and an hundred of you shall put ten thousand to flight. Covenantal asymmetry: blessing-fueled victory.
John.19.30: It is finished (tetelestai — perfect tense): and He bowed His head, and gave up the ghost. The completed atonement in one Greek word.
Heb.6.4: It is impossible for those who were once enlightened... if they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance. The warning passage. Genuinely converted believers persevere; apostates demonstrate they were never truly converted.
Luke.12.32: Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom. The Father's delight in giving the kingdom.
Mal.3.1: Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me. Cited Mark 1:2 of John the Baptist.
2Tim.4.17: Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known. Divine sustaining at Paul's first defense.
1John.4.10: Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that He loved us, and sent His Son to be the propitiation (hilasmos) for our sins. Wrath-appeasing atonement defines love.
Jer.20.11: The LORD is with me as a mighty terrible one: therefore my persecutors shall stumble. Divine partisanship for the persecuted prophet.
1Pet.2.2: As newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word, that ye may grow thereby. Word-as-milk for new converts.
Acts.17.24: God that made the world and all things therein, seeing that He is Lord of heaven and earth, dwelleth not in temples made with hands. Paul's Areopagus address — God's transcendence over idolatry.
Ps.91.14: Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him: I will set him on high, because he hath known my name. Personal devotion eliciting divine deliverance.
2Pet.2.9: The Lord knoweth how to deliver the godly out of temptations, and to reserve the unjust unto the day of judgment. Twin divine capacities — rescue and reserve.
Eph.5.1: Be ye therefore followers (mimētai — imitators) of God, as dear children. Familial imitation of God.
Ezek.34.23: I will set up one shepherd over them, and he shall feed them, even my servant David. Davidic-Messianic prophecy of one shepherd.
Matt.22.39: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. The second great commandment.
1Pet.1.5: Kept by the power of God through faith unto salvation ready to be revealed in the last time. Divine guarding of the elect via faith.
Matt.5.48: Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect. The standard is the Father's character.
1Thess.5.15: See that none render evil for evil unto any man; but ever follow that which is good. Non-retaliation universalized.
2Cor.12.10: When I am weak, then am I strong. The Christian paradox of strength-through-weakness.
Gal.4.6: Because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of His Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father. Spirit's adoption-cry.
