Ps.139.1: O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me. Divine omniscience opens the most personal psalm.
1Pet.3.12: The eyes of the Lord are over the righteous, and His ears are open unto their prayers. Cites Ps 34:15.
Isa.45.24: In the LORD have I righteousness and strength: even to Him shall men come. Pre-figuring justification by faith.
1John.5.19: We know that we are of God, and the whole world lieth in wickedness (en tō ponērō). Two spheres — believer's identity vs. cosmic darkness.
Hos.1.10: It shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people, there it shall be said unto them, Ye are the sons of the living God. Cited Rom 9:26 — Gentile inclusion.
1Cor.3.9: Ye are God's husbandry, ye are God's building. Two metaphors for the church.
1John.2.6: He that saith he abideth in Him ought himself also so to walk, even as He walked. The Christian life conformed to Christ's pattern.
1John.2.16: All that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father. The world's three primary lusts.
Eph.1.19: What is the exceeding greatness of His power to us-ward who believe. Hyperbolic divine power available to believers.
1John.4.18: Perfect love casteth out fear. Mature love eliminates the fear of judgment.
Jer.10.23: O LORD, I know that the way of man is not in himself: it is not in man that walketh to direct his steps. Human autonomy is illusory.
Jas.1.14: Every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed. Internal lust as the engine of temptation.
Deut.8.2: Thou shalt remember all the way which the LORD thy God led thee these forty years... to humble thee, and to prove thee. Wilderness as covenantal pedagogy.
Hos.2.23: I will say to them which were not my people, Thou art my people. Cited Rom 9:25. Gentile gathering.
Matt.13.44: The kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath. The kingdom's incomparable value.
Gen.1.3: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light. Divine speech as creative power. Cited 2 Cor 4:6 of regeneration.
Isa.42.16: I will bring the blind by a way that they knew not... I will make darkness light before them. Divine guidance for the spiritually blind.
Rom.11.36: Of Him, and through Him, and to Him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever. Doxological compression of divine sovereignty.
Rev.19.1: Alleluia; Salvation, and glory, and honour, and power, unto the Lord our God. Heaven's eschatological doxology.
1Pet.4.12: Think it not strange concerning the fiery trial which is to try you, as though some strange thing happened. Suffering is normative for Christians.
Rev.17.14: The Lamb shall overcome them: for He is Lord of lords, and King of kings. Eschatological victory of Christ.
Ps.119.97: O how love I thy law! it is my meditation all the day. Affection for Scripture, sustained meditation.
Isa.32.15: Until the Spirit be poured upon us from on high. Eschatological Spirit-effusion. Cited at Pentecost typologically.
1John.5.3: This is the love of God, that we keep His commandments: and His commandments are not grievous (bareiai). Love defined by obedience; commandments characterized as not heavy.
Ps.118.22: The stone which the builders refused is become the head stone of the corner. Cited five times in NT of Christ.
Rev.1.18: I am He that liveth, and was dead; and, behold, I am alive for evermore. Christ's death-and-resurrection witnessed.
Ps.23.6: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for ever. Pursuit by divine attributes; eternal dwelling.
1Cor.3.18: Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise. Inversion of worldly wisdom.
Isa.11.1: There shall come forth a rod out of the stem of Jesse, and a Branch shall grow out of his roots. Davidic-Messianic prophecy.
Matt.13.14: By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand. Cites Isa 6:9. Judicial hardening.
1Pet.2.6: Behold, I lay in Sion a chief corner stone, elect, precious. Cites Isa 28:16 of Christ.
2Cor.6.17: Wherefore come out from among them, and be ye separate, saith the Lord. Cited from Isa 52:11. Holiness as separation.
Matt.7.12: Whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them. The Golden Rule. Sums the Law and Prophets.
Isa.43.5: Fear not: for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west. Eschatological regathering.
Ps.91.2: I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in Him will I trust. Personal confession of refuge.
Isa.50.4: The Lord GOD hath given me the tongue of the learned, that I should know how to speak a word in season to him that is weary. The Servant's pastoral speech.
Ps.102.13: Thou shalt arise, and have mercy upon Zion: for the time to favour her, yea, the set time, is come. Eschatological mercy at the appointed time.
John.17.17: Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth. Word-mediated sanctification.
1Pet.4.1: He that hath suffered in the flesh hath ceased from sin. Suffering as a sin-killing discipline.
Luke.21.15: I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist. Christ's promise of speech-aid in persecution.
Deut.7.6: Thou art an holy people unto the LORD thy God: the LORD thy God hath chosen thee to be a special people. Election plus holiness-by-set-apartness.
Matt.24.24: There shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect. Eschatological deception attempt.
1Tim.2.5: There is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus. Singularity of mediation through the God-Man.
Luke.16.15: Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God. Divine valuation inverts human valuation.
Gal.6.14: God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world. Cross-only glorying.
Eph.2.5: Even when we were dead in sins, hath quickened us together with Christ, (by grace ye are saved). Spiritual resurrection paralleled with Christ's.
Prov.3.7: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil. Anti-self-confidence wisdom.
2Tim.4.2: Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season. Pastoral mandate — preach regardless of cultural reception.
Phil.3.13: This one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind, and reaching forth unto those things which are before. Singular focus, athletic forward press.
John.9.31: God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth His will, him He heareth. The healed man's theological clarity.
Rom.5.20: Where sin abounded, grace did much more abound. Grace's superabundance over sin.
Matt.11.5: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk... and the poor have the gospel preached to them. Christ's messianic credentials cited from Isaiah.
Job.23.12: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of His lips; I have esteemed the words of His mouth more than my necessary food. Word-prioritization above sustenance.
2Cor.3.3: Ye are manifestly declared to be the epistle of Christ ministered by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God. Believers as living letters of Christ.
Ps.56.3: What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee. Faith as the answer to fear.
Matt.13.11: Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given. Sovereign distribution of revelation.
Heb.9.15: For this cause He is the mediator of the new testament, that... they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance. Christ's mediation underwriting eternal inheritance.
Rom.8.17: If children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with Him, that we may be also glorified together. Inheritance via co-suffering and co-glory with Christ.
Joel.2.12: Turn ye even to me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning. Whole-person repentance.
John.20.31: These are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ... and that believing ye might have life through His name. The Gospel of John's stated purpose.
Ps.138.7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, thou wilt revive me. Divine revival amid trouble.
Gen.18.19: I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the LORD. Election manifesting in generational obedience-passing.
Jer.31.3: I have loved thee with an everlasting love: therefore with lovingkindness have I drawn thee. Eternal love as the source of effectual drawing.
Mark.11.23: Whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed... and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe... he shall have whatsoever he saith. Faith-prayer's mountain-moving capacity.
Ps.103.17: The mercy of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear Him. Eternal-temporal mercy span for the God-fearing.
Luke.14.11: Whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted. Pride-humility reversal in the kingdom.
Matt.4.10: Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and Him only shalt thou serve. Christ's wilderness rebuke citing Deut 6:13.
Mal.1.6: A son honoureth his father, and a servant his master: if then I be a father, where is mine honour? Lawsuit indictment against Israel's priests.
2Cor.4.8: We are troubled on every side, yet not distressed; we are perplexed, but not in despair. The paradoxical resilience of cross-shaped life.
Col.2.8: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit. Anti-syncretism warning. Christ's sufficiency vs. human speculation.
1Thess.4.1: How ye ought to walk and to please God, so ye would abound more and more. Continual increase in pleasing-God.
John.4.24: God is a Spirit: and they that worship Him must worship Him in spirit and in truth. The qualitative requirements for true worship.
Mark.16.17: These signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues. Apostolic signs accompanying gospel preaching.
John.14.17: Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth Him not, neither knoweth Him: but ye know Him; for He dwelleth with you, and shall be in you. Spirit-distinction between believers and the world.
Rev.22.12: Behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be. Christ's imminent return with reward.
John.15.10: If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and abide in His love. Obedience-sustained love-abiding.
Ezek.33.31: They sit before thee as my people... but they hear thy words, and they do them not. Hypocritical hearing without obedience.
Rev.1.17: Fear not; I am the first and the last. The risen Christ's self-assertion in divine titles.
Deut.20.4: The LORD your God is He that goeth with you, to fight for you against your enemies. Pre-battle assurance of divine partisanship.
Eph.5.17: Be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord is. Wise discernment of God's will as Christian responsibility.
Luke.2.11: Unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord. The angelic announcement compressing the gospel.
Deut.32.4: He is the Rock, His work is perfect: for all His ways are judgment: a God of truth and without iniquity. Divine moral perfection in poetic compression.
Prov.1.23: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you. Wisdom's offered Spirit-effusion at repentance.
Luke.18.14: This man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased. The publican-Pharisee parable's verdict.
Lam.3.23: They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness. Cited in the famous hymn. Daily-renewing mercies.
Titus.2.12: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly. Grace's pedagogical effect.
Phil.3.21: Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto His glorious body. Eschatological resurrection-glorification.
Eph.2.20: Built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ Himself being the chief corner stone. Apostolic doctrine plus Christological keystone.
Matt.17.20: If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove. Faith's quality, not quantity.
1Pet.3.22: Who is gone into heaven, and is on the right hand of God; angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto Him. Christ's exaltation over all angelic powers.
Eccl.12.14: God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing. Eschatological accountability of all hidden things.
Rev.7.9: Behold, a great multitude, which no man could number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, stood before the throne. Eschatological congregation — universal, particular, redeemed.
Isa.26.4: Trust ye in the LORD for ever: for in the LORD JEHOVAH is everlasting strength (tsur olamim — Rock of Ages). Eternal Rock-trust.
Acts.2.42: They continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers. Four marks of the early church.
Eph.3.17: That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith; that ye, being rooted and grounded in love. Heart-Christology by faith.
Isa.66.1: The heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool: where is the house that ye build unto me? God's transcendence over any temple.
Ps.32.1: Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered. Cited Rom 4:7. Imputed-righteousness blessing.
Isa.52.7: How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him that bringeth good tidings. Cited Rom 10:15 of gospel preachers.
1Cor.8.6: To us there is but one God, the Father, of whom are all things... and one Lord Jesus Christ, by whom are all things. Compressed Christological monotheism.
Luke.23.43: To day shalt thou be with me in paradise. Christ's word to the repentant thief. Conscious post-mortem fellowship with Christ.
John.12.35: Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you. Urgency of response to Christ.
Phil.3.14: I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus. Athletic forward-pressing toward eschatological prize.
Prov.30.5: Every word of God is pure: He is a shield unto them that put their trust in Him. Verbal inspiration plus protective trust.
Matt.7.11: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask Him? A fortiori argument from human to divine fatherhood.
John.14.12: He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do. Greater in scope (the gospel goes to all nations), not greater in supernatural display.
Jas.5.11: Ye have heard of the patience of Job, and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very pitiful, and of tender mercy. Job as model of patient endurance.
Ps.33.6: By the word of the LORD were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of His mouth. Verbal creation-power.
Deut.20.1: When thou goest out to battle against thine enemies, and seest horses, and chariots... be not afraid of them: for the LORD thy God is with thee. Pre-battle non-fear command grounded in divine presence.
Rom.5.10: If, when we were enemies, we were reconciled to God by the death of His Son, much more, being reconciled, we shall be saved by His life. Reconciliation past; preservation by Christ's life present-future.
Rom.6.13: Yield (paristanete) yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness. Resurrection-life conditioned self-presentation.
John.15.13: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends. Self-giving as love's apex.
Eph.2.1: You hath He quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins. Spiritual death the pre-conversion state; quickening the divine act.
Rev.21.7: He that overcometh shall inherit all things; and I will be his God, and he shall be my son. Covenant formula plus eschatological inheritance.
Dan.9.24: Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people... to make an end of sins, and to make reconciliation for iniquity. The seventy-weeks prophecy with Messianic culmination.
Eph.5.20: Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ. Comprehensive thanksgiving.
2Cor.3.6: The letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life. The Mosaic letter condemns; the new-covenant Spirit gives life.
Rom.12.18: If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men. Realistic peace-pursuit — qualified by 'if possible.'
Phil.2.5: Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus. Christ-mindedness anchored to the Christ-hymn (vv. 6–11).
2Tim.2.25: In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance. Pastoral correction with hope of God-given repentance.
Gal.3.26: Ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus. Sonship by faith, not by works.
1Tim.4.8: Bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come. Godliness's two-realm dividend.
Jer.32.39: I will give them one heart, and one way, that they may fear me for ever. New-covenant unity-of-heart promise.
Rom.8.24: We are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope. Eschatological hope as a salvation-component.
Isa.41.17: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none... I the LORD will hear them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them. Divine response to the destitute.
Rev.3.7: He that is holy, He that is true, He that hath the key of David, He that openeth, and no man shutteth. Christ's sovereign mission-door management.
Ps.40.1: I waited patiently for the LORD; and He inclined unto me, and heard my cry. Cited Heb 10:5–7 of Christ.
Rom.1.21: When they knew God, they glorified Him not as God, neither were thankful. The root of human depravity: dishonor and ingratitude.
Acts.7.51: Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost. Stephen's indictment of Israel's Spirit-resistance.
Ps.86.5: Thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee. Divine readiness to forgive.
Deut.6.7: Thou shalt teach them diligently unto thy children, and shalt talk of them when thou sittest in thine house. The Shema's pedagogical extension. Constant Word-conversation in family life.
Eph.5.6: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience. Coming wrath as the corrective to deceptive moral relativism.
Isa.35.3: Strengthen ye the weak hands, and confirm the feeble knees. Cited Heb 12:12. Encouragement of the discouraged.
2Thess.2.9: Whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders. The Antichrist's satanic empowerment.
Rom.6.14: Sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under the law, but under grace. Grace as the regime of victory over sin.
Gen.2.7: The LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul. Divine personal involvement in human creation.
Matt.18.19: If two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask, it shall be done for them. Corporate prayer's special promise.
Matt.2.6: Thou Bethlehem... art not the least among the princes of Judah: for out of thee shall come a Governor. The chief priests' citation of Mic 5:2.
Exod.20.5: I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children. Divine jealousy and generational consequence of idolatry.
Col.2.9: In Him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily. Christ's full deity in human flesh — the strongest possible incarnational statement.
Luke.12.15: Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth. Anti-covetousness life-principle.
Phil.3.12: Not as though I had already attained, either were already perfect: but I follow after. Sanctification's incompleteness even for Paul.
Jas.3.2: In many things we offend all. If any man offend not in word, the same is a perfect man. Speech-control as the mark of maturity.
1Cor.2.7: We speak the wisdom of God in a mystery... which God ordained before the world unto our glory. Pre-temporal wisdom now revealed.
Prov.22.6: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it. Pedagogical principle for parenting.
Phil.2.7: Made Himself of no reputation (heauton ekenōsen — emptied Himself), and took upon Him the form of a servant. The kenosis — voluntary self-emptying for incarnation.
Ps.46.11: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Repeated refrain of Psalm 46.
Heb.12.15: Lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you. Cites Deut 29:18. Bitterness as a contagious moral root.
Acts.16.31: Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved, and thy house. Conversion's offer; household evangelism principle.
Rev.3.20: Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in. Christ's persistent knocking at the church's door.
Matt.5.12: Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven. Eschatological reward as the source of present rejoicing.
Rom.5.12: By one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin... for that all have sinned. Federal headship of Adam.
Mark.10.27: With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible. Salvation impossible to humans, possible to God.
John.17.24: Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory. Christ's high-priestly request for the elect.
John.12.26: If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be. Servant-following promised eternal companionship.
Ps.37.25: I have been young, and now am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread. Davidic observation of covenantal provision.
Jer.30.17: For I will restore health unto thee, and I will heal thee of thy wounds, saith the LORD. Eschatological healing of Israel.
1Pet.4.13: Rejoice, inasmuch as ye are partakers of Christ's sufferings; that, when His glory shall be revealed, ye may be glad also with exceeding joy. Cross-fellowship producing eschatological joy.
Ezek.34.11: Behold, I, even I, will both search my sheep, and seek them out. Yahweh's personal pastoral search after Israel's faithless shepherds fail.
Ps.130.5: I wait for the LORD, my soul doth wait, and in His word do I hope. Triple waiting-language tied to Word-rooted hope.
Prov.8.17: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me. Wisdom's promise to early-seekers.
